items.name: Iron axe
items.examine: Heavier than bronze. The trees have started taking it personally.

items.name: Steel axe
items.examine: A proper feller's axe. It finishes the chord a beat sooner.

items.name: Mithril axe
items.examine: Pale blue and light in the hand. The swing whistles a clean third.

items.name: Adamant axe
items.examine: Green-flecked and unsentimental. Timber falls just to get it over with.

items.name: Rune axe
items.examine: Storm-grey and certain. Bole sells these the way other men sell heirlooms.

items.name: Resonant axe
items.examine: The head is tuned to the Chordwood's key. Each cut ends on a small, satisfied chime.

items.name: Charcoal
items.examine: A log that has been talked out of being a log. The note is gone; the heat stayed.

items.name: Sap-glass lantern
items.examine: Chordwood sap, fired to glass around a wick. It hums very faintly in B-flat.

items.name: Forester's jerkin
items.examine: Sawdust-gold leather. The pockets are full of pockets, which are full of sawdust.

items.name: Forester's breeches
items.examine: Reinforced at the knee for kneeling beside stumps and apologising.

items.name: Forester's cap
items.examine: Keeps the sun off and the resin out. Mostly the resin.

items.name: Ashen tabard
items.examine: Sap-singed at every seam, and the seams glow when the bonfire does.

items.name: Ashen hood
items.examine: Smells of woodsmoke no matter how it is washed. Ysolde calls this a feature.

items.name: Chordwood logs
items.examine: The grain rings when tapped. Somewhere inside, a note is waiting for a fire.

items.name: Chorale token
items.examine: A sap-amber disc stamped with one bar of music. Ysolde redeems them; nobody else can read them.

objs.name: Chordwood tree
objs.examine: Resonant timber. It has been holding the same chord since Syla sang it up; it would like to be excused.

objs.name: Emberyard bonfire
objs.examine: A pooled chord of burning logs, decades deep. Warm enough to cook on, loud enough to pay you for the privilege.

objs.name: Charcoal pit
objs.examine: Quill's earth-banked smoulder. Logs go in singing and come out quiet, black, and useful.

npcs.name: Bole the Feller
npcs.examine: Sells axes in every metal. Names each one, then sells it anyway.

npcs.name: Cinder-Warden Ysolde
npcs.examine: Keeps the great bonfire lit and the chorale tokens honest. Smells permanently of warm resin.

npcs.name: Sawwright Tamsin
npcs.examine: Runs the waterwheel sawmill. Claims it keeps better time than the temple metronome.

npcs.name: Old Quill the Coalman
npcs.examine: Minds the charcoal pit. Black to the elbows, philosophical to the brim.

sharedEdits.what: GATHER_REQS (~line 694): add the four missing/new Woodcutting keys so the generic gather handler resolves maple/yew/magic_tree (already in skillObjs) and the new chordwood. tool stays 'bronze_axe' as the legacy floor; the Woodcutting branch added below (AXES sharedEdit) supersedes the named-tool check.
sharedEdits.snippet:
maple: { skill: 'Woodcutting', tool: 'bronze_axe' },
  yew: { skill: 'Woodcutting', tool: 'bronze_axe' },
  magic_tree: { skill: 'Woodcutting', tool: 'bronze_axe' },
  chordwood: { skill: 'Woodcutting', tool: 'bronze_axe' },

sharedEdits.what: FIREMAKING array (~line 710): append the missing tiers so every cut log is burnable. Values follow the existing chop->burn ratio; chordwood inserts at FM 65 by design (reward log).
sharedEdits.snippet:
{ log: 'maple_logs', level: 45, xp: 135 },
  { log: 'yew_logs', level: 60, xp: 202.5 },
  { log: 'chordwood_logs', level: 65, xp: 240 },
  { log: 'magic_logs', level: 75, xp: 303.75 },

sharedEdits.what: FIREMAKING array (~line 144): append the SAME four rows as the server table (the two arrays are separate hardcoded copies and must stay 1:1).
sharedEdits.snippet:
{ log: 'maple_logs', level: 45, xp: 135 },
  { log: 'yew_logs', level: 60, xp: 202.5 },
  { log: 'chordwood_logs', level: 65, xp: 240 },
  { log: 'magic_logs', level: 75, xp: 303.75 },

sharedEdits.what: AXE tier ladder: add an AXES table + axeBonus() directly below PICKAXES/pickaxeBonus (~line 268), modelled exactly on the pickaxe ladder (any axe lets you chop; the best one you qualify for adds a flat success bonus).
gather() tool branch (~line 303): wire axeBonus the same way pickaxeBonus is wired — insert a Woodcutting branch between the Mining branch and the generic reqs.tool fallback.

Woodcutting loop (~line 65): the client gate hardcodes hasTool('bronze_axe'); higher axes must satisfy it. Add the axe list + helper above the loop and replace the bronze_axe check inside the tree/oak/willow handler.

maple/yew/magic_tree loop (~line 42): same client-side fix — replace hasTool('bronze_axe') with a hasAxe() helper over the seven axe ids (or import hasAnyAxe from src/packs/resin_hollow.ts, which exports it).
sharedEdits.snippet:
import { hasAnyAxe } from './resin_hollow';
// ...in the handler, replace:  if (!hasTool('bronze_axe'))  with:  if (!hasAnyAxe())

sharedEdits.what: cook() station guard (~line 376): accept a fire or the Emberyard bonfire as a cook station, not just a range. NOTE: personal fires are client-cosmetic objects, so only map-baked 'fire'/'bonfire' objects pass the server index — the Emberyard bonfire is baked and works; this keeps the OR future-proof for baked fires.
Register the Resin Hollow client pack (import for side effects).
sharedEdits.snippet: import './resin_hollow';        // Resin Hollow — Chordwood grove + Emberyard (woodcutting/firemaking hub)

sharedEdits.what: Register the forestry intent domain beside the other intent-domain imports (~line 41).
sharedEdits.snippet: import './intent-forestry'; // side-effect: registers bonfire/quench/forestry-buy domain intents

sharedEdits.what: buildObjectTemplate (~line 749): cases for the three new object types so they don't render as the fallback crate, plus add 'chordwood' to NATURAL_RE (~line 1386) and TREE_FALL_RE (~line 2861).
